headline: PRE-OPEN — Chip complex snaps back after 2-session Fri/Thu sell; Nasdaq futures +1.05% into open, SMH ETF +2.7% at open; INTC +3% pre-market to $123.98, AMD/MU/AVGO all indicated higher; Dow futures +0.06% as defensive rotation cools; setup framed by SoftBank SB Neo weekend news + SpaceX Nasdaq-100 add pre-Tuesday
summary: Monday July 6, 2026 pre-open — US equity futures pointing to a broad tech rebound on the first trading day since Thursday's chip-led Nasdaq -0.82% close. Nasdaq-100 futures +1.05% (+310.50 pts), S&P 500 futures +0.48% (+36.00 pts), Dow futures +0.06% (+33.00 pts) — the relative spread shows sector rotation flipping back INTO tech and OUT of defensives that led Thursday's Dow record. VanEck Semiconductor ETF SMH +2.7% at the opening bell. Notable pre-market chip moves: INTC +3% to $123.98 (foundry momentum + government-stake tailwind), AMD indicated higher after Cantor Fitzgerald's $700 top-of-Street PT last week, MU higher into Wednesday's fiscal Q4 read. Tesla +1.14% pre-market on Miami Robotaxi launch (Florida = third state). Two setup drivers for the week: (1) SoftBank SB Neo weekend news reframes the neocloud complex — negative for CRWV/NBIS/IREN, positive for NVDA/VRT/ETN/GEV (see paired entry); (2) SpaceX (SPCX) enters Nasdaq-100 pre-open Tuesday July 7 — up to $4.3B QQQ forced buy on a 3-5% float. Read-through to House coverage: (a) risk-on tone favors the semi/DC-hardware cohort (NVDA, AMD, MU, AVGO, ANET, VRT, DELL, SMCI); (b) rotation OUT of defensives is a modest headwind for the regulated-utility book (NEE, AES) but should NOT hit the merchant-power/nuclear names (CEG, VST, SMR, OKLO, LEU, CCJ) which trade with AI-demand narrative; (c) any Section 232 semiconductor report drop this week is the biggest downside binary.

headline: SOFTBANK LAUNCHES SB NEO — new US neocloud subsidiary incorporated in Delaware July 2026 (51% SBK Corp / 49% SBG Corp) targeting 10-GW US AI-datacenter capacity by ~2030; anchor site is Portsmouth AI Technology Campus in Pike County, Ohio on former DOE gaseous diffusion (uranium enrichment) land leased to SB Energy; commercial service go-live in FY2027 (ending March 2028) using proprietary Infrinia AI Cloud OS already beta in Japan
summary: SoftBank Corp. and SoftBank Group Corp. jointly announced Thursday July 2 the formation of SB Neo, Inc., a new US neocloud subsidiary incorporated in Delaware in July 2026, 51% owned by SoftBank Corp. and 49% by SoftBank Group Corp. (consolidated under SBK Corp.). Bloomberg, IBTimes JP and DCD all confirmed. Target: 10 gigawatts of deployed AI datacenter capacity by around 2030 — roughly equal to a mid-size US state's peak grid load, and 3x CoreWeave's current contracted power footprint. Service launch scheduled for FY2027 (fiscal year ending March 31, 2028) providing US enterprises and hyperscalers with GPU compute for large-scale AI training and inference. Anchor site: Portsmouth AI Technology Campus at the former Portsmouth Gaseous Diffusion Plant in Pike County, Ohio — Cold-War-era uranium enrichment facility leased from DOE, with SB Energy (SoftBank's energy sub) developing on-site gas-fired power to route around interconnection queues. Software: proprietary 'Infrinia AI Cloud OS' stack, in beta service in Japan since May 2026. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MATERIAL NEGATIVE for CRWV/NBIS/IREN — SB Neo is the second major new neocloud entrant in a week (Meta Compute the other) and directly attacks the growth-optionality narrative that already sold off 14-17% on July 1; CRWV now -48% from its $166 high; (2) POSITIVE for NVDA — SB Neo means yet another aggressive hyperscale-tier GPU buyer, not less silicon demand; (3) POSITIVE for VRT/ETN/GEV — 10 GW of new hyperscaler capacity absorbs power/cooling equipment regardless of who monetizes the compute; (4) POSITIVE for LEU (Centrus, whose Piketon Ohio ACC/HALEU plant is 20 miles from the Portsmouth AI campus and shares the DOE-land provenance) — a materially different demand narrative may emerge if the Portsmouth site draws HALEU-fueled microreactors as a power complement to gas; (5) NEUTRAL for hyperscalers (MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META/ORCL) — new competitor but at 2028+ scale.

headline: SAMSUNG (005930.KS) — Q2 preliminary earnings due Tuesday July 7; sell-side consensus ~KRW 85.5T operating profit (~$55.9B), revenue ~KRW 169.4T (~$110.7B), a 17-18x YoY OP surge driven by HBM/memory + AI-server pull; Samsung shares +3.34% in Seoul Monday to KRW 325,000 as KOSPI +1.22% on the print anticipation; brokerages range KRW 80T-92T with performance-bonus provisioning the main dispersion driver
summary: Samsung Electronics is scheduled to release its Q2 2026 preliminary earnings figure Tuesday July 7 (Korea open Tue AM = Mon evening US). Preliminary release is guidance-only — no divisional breakdown — but sets tone for the full print due July 23. FnGuide sell-side consensus: KRW 169.4T revenue (~$110.7B) and KRW 85.5T operating profit (~$55.9B), which would be a 17-18x YoY OP surge from Q2 2025's KRW 4.7T. Brokerage estimate range KRW 80-92T with the dispersion primarily driven by different assumptions about performance-bonus provisions. Key drivers: HBM3E/HBM4 pricing + volume, DDR5 server DRAM ramp, foundry loss narrowing on 2nm sample runs. Management full-year OP guidance running near KRW 300T (~$200B). Market positioning heading in: Samsung +3.34% to KRW 325,000 Monday in Seoul, SK Hynix +1.98% to KRW 2,473,000, KOSPI +1.22% to 8,186.82. Read-through to House coverage: (1) BINARY EVENT for MU — a Samsung print at or above KRW 90T OP would validate the memory-supercycle framing that already drove MU +305% YTD; a print below KRW 80T would trigger a competitor-margin-guard-down worry into MU's fiscal-Q4 print later this quarter; (2) POSITIVE for 000660.KS (SK Hynix) heading into its July 10 Nasdaq ADR debut ('SKHY') at $166 priced ($29.65B raise) — a strong Samsung print re-rates the whole HBM complex; (3) READ-THROUGH for the fab-equipment complex (ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, 8035.T Tokyo Electron) — Samsung's Q2 capex commentary is a leading indicator for the $518B Korean memory-fab commitment announced July 3.

headline: TESLA (TSLA) — Robotaxi service goes live in Miami Monday July 6, 2026; third US state (after Texas/California) with commercial autonomous ride-hailing; TSLA +1.14% pre-market; service confined to western + central Miami, excludes downtown and Miami Beach; expansion arrives 4 days after Q2 deliveries print at record 480,126 (+25% YoY, +34% Q/Q) that crushed 401K/406K consensus but saw the stock drop 7.49% on 'sell-the-news' + robotaxi expectations reset
summary: Tesla announced pre-open Monday July 6, 2026 that its Robotaxi service is now live in Miami, Florida — the third US state to receive commercial autonomous ride-hailing after Austin (June 2025) and California (Bay Area, spring 2026). Initial coverage area is limited to western and central Miami, deliberately excluding downtown corridors and Miami Beach where the pedestrian-density and cruise-ship-drop-off patterns create the toughest edge cases. TSLA shares +1.14% pre-market on the news. Backdrop: Tesla reported Q2 2026 deliveries of 480,126 vehicles on Thursday July 2 — up 25% YoY and 34% Q/Q, and roughly 74,000 vehicles above the ~406K Wall Street consensus and ~401K sell-side average. It was Tesla's strongest Q2 ever and its first YoY delivery growth after two straight years of decline. Despite the crush, the stock dropped 7.49% on the delivery day as (a) the print was already largely leaked through July 1 registration data, (b) analyst PTs converged around Barclays' Equal-Weight $360 / JPMorgan Underweight $115 leaving little forward upside, and (c) some sell-siders reset robotaxi expansion cadence expectations. Miami is a partial rebuttal to (c). Q2 earnings due July 22. Read-through: modest positive on the day for TSLA and by-extension for the autonomy-adjacent thesis around GOOGL/Waymo comparables; not thesis-altering for the AI-infrastructure coverage.

headline: SPACEX (SPCX) — enters Nasdaq-100 pre-open Tuesday July 7 via the new fast-track rule (top-40 mkt cap post-IPO = 15-trading-day eligibility, effective May 1, 2026); passive-fund forced buying est. $4.3B QQQ alone, up to ~$27B across all Nasdaq-100 / Russell trackers; only 3-5% of shares in float creates tight supply/demand imbalance; SPCX +1.08% Monday pre-market to $163.75; fastest major-index inclusion after IPO in history
summary: SpaceX (SPCX) will be added to the Nasdaq-100 before the market opens Tuesday July 7, 2026, forcing passive tracking-fund managers to complete forced buys ahead of the open. Nasdaq's new fast-track rule (effective May 1, 2026) allows any company ranked in the top 40 by market cap at the time of IPO to enter the Nasdaq-100 after just 15 trading days; SpaceX IPO'd June 12 at $135/share valuing the company at approximately $1.75T. Estimated forced buying: Invesco QQQ alone ~$4.3B, total across all Nasdaq-100 and adjacent Russell trackers up to ~$27B. The wrinkle: only 3-5% of SPCX shares are freely tradable (Musk + private pre-IPO holders retain the balance), creating a tight supply/demand imbalance that could produce outsize moves in the July 7 opening auction. SPCX +1.08% Monday pre-market to $163.75. This is the fastest major-index inclusion after IPO in modern market history. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MECHANICAL positive for Nasdaq-100 index level — the SPCX add is a net add (no removal announced) which pushes the benchmark up regardless of underlying tech-cohort action; (2) modest MIX-SHIFT drag for every existing QQQ constituent as the passive weights get re-cut to accommodate SPCX; heaviest bleed for the top-weight names (MSFT, AAPL, NVDA, GOOGL, META) and by extension for the House-covered mega-caps MSFT/GOOGL/META/AMZN/NVDA; (3) index momentum should sustain the Monday risk-on tone into Tuesday.

headline: OKLO (OKLO) — Groves isotope test reactor did NOT achieve criticality over the July 4 long-weekend window per available reporting; DOE-approved Documented Safety Analysis (July 1) remains the most recent milestone; Oklo/Atomic Alchemy still guiding 'criticality in July' with DOE readiness review + fuel loading remaining ahead of first self-sustaining chain reaction; long weekend passed without either a criticality release or a slippage disclosure
summary: Oklo and its Atomic Alchemy subsidiary did NOT confirm first criticality of the Groves Isotope Test Reactor over the July 3-5 long-holiday weekend, per available public reporting through Monday July 6 pre-open. The most recent milestone remains the July 1 DOE approval of the Documented Safety Analysis (DSA), which the company had positioned as the last major regulatory hurdle before startup. Company's own X-account (Oklo/@oklo) still guides 'criticality in July' as of the July 1 post; Foreign Policy Journal (Jul 4) and Interesting Engineering both frame the target window as 'July 2026' rather than specifically the July 4 US-250th-anniversary milestone that had been rumoured. Remaining path: (1) DOE readiness review; (2) DOE startup approval; (3) fuel load; (4) startup testing; (5) first self-sustaining chain reaction. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEUTRAL-TO-SLIGHTLY-NEGATIVE for OKLO — the July 4 miss removes a near-term catalyst, but the DSA approval already de-risks the primary short thesis and any Q3 criticality release remains structurally positive; (2) NEUTRAL for SMR and X-energy (XE) — the SMR/microreactor complex trades as a group; no change to competitive positioning; (3) monitor for a Tuesday/Wednesday DOE-readiness-review headline as the next binary event.

headline: BLOOM ENERGY (BE) — Brookfield partnership scale-up to $25B total commitment ratified June 30 (5x original $5B framework) as AI datacenter operators route around utility interconnection queues via on-site solid-oxide fuel cells; commitment underwrites several years of Bloom's stated 2-GW annual production-capacity target; readacross to Oracle Project Jupiter (BorderPlex) showing SOFC-on-site is now the primary commercial route to <18-month datacenter power
summary: Reminder heading into Monday July 6 open: Bloom Energy and Brookfield disclosed June 30 that they have expanded their fuel-cell AI-power partnership framework to $25B total commitment, 5x the original $5B announced earlier in 2026, per DCD, TechTimes and EnkiAI coverage. The scale-up reflects (1) AI datacenter operators now routing structurally AROUND grid interconnection queues that in most PJM/ERCOT/CAISO zones now exceed 48 months by opting for on-site solid-oxide fuel cells (SOFCs) delivering 16-24-month time-to-power, and (2) Bloom's 2-GW annual production capacity target now being fully underwritten by hyperscaler-backed offtake through 2028. Parallel Bloom wins: Oracle Project Jupiter (with BorderPlex) already under contract for on-site SOFC deployment. Read-through to House coverage: (1) STRUCTURAL POSITIVE for BE — the Brookfield partnership converts growth-stage optionality into contracted revenue and re-rates BE closer to a utility-comparable multiple; (2) POSITIVE for FCEL (FuelCell Energy) by category re-rating (Jefferies upgrade Jun 27 already reflected this, PT $16→$24 Hold-to-Buy); (3) SLIGHT NEGATIVE at the margin for CEG/VST/NEE — every GW of behind-the-meter SOFC is a GW that does NOT show up as merchant offtake, though the total-addressable-market growth rate is fast enough that the offset is small in absolute terms.

headline: POLICY OVERHANGS INTACT INTO MONDAY OPEN — (1) Section 232 semiconductor-datacenter market report Commerce Sec. Lutnick owed Trump by July 1 STILL not publicly released as of Monday pre-open; (2) OpenAI 5% US-government-equity-stake proposal (FT/Bloomberg July 2) sits in early-stage discussion with Trump/Lutnick/Bessent, congressional action still needed for implementation; both remain material binary events for the semi cohort with no timeline visibility
summary: Two policy overhangs from Thursday's session carry into Monday's US open without resolution over the long weekend. (1) SECTION 232 SEMICONDUCTOR-DATACENTER MARKET REPORT — Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick was required under President Trump's January 14 Proclamation 11002 to deliver Trump an updated market study on the US datacenter semiconductor supply chain by July 1, 2026. That deadline passed without a public release; no press briefing or leaked-highlights coverage over the July 3-5 window. The report gives Trump statutory authority to (a) leave the current 25% Section 232 tariff on NVDA H200 / AMD MI325X-class chips untouched, (b) raise or expand it to cover more product codes, (c) pair it with new licensing conditions on China-destined shipments, or (d) create carveouts for domestic supply-chain buildout. (2) OPENAI 5% US-GOVERNMENT-STAKE PROPOSAL — FT and Bloomberg both confirmed Wednesday-into-Thursday that Sam Altman is in early-stage 'conceptual' discussions with Trump / Lutnick / Bessent to grant the US government a 5% equity stake (~$42.6B at OpenAI's $852B mark) in each leading US AI lab (Anthropic, GOOGL, META, NVDA, MU, AMD reportedly named). Structure modeled on Alaska Permanent Fund. Congressional action required. Read-through to House coverage: MATERIAL BINARY OVERHANG persists for NVDA/AMD/INTC/MU into the Wednesday-Thursday session as the market prices two possible-but-not-timed policy shocks; the July 6 chip rebound suggests positioning is currently long the tape but light on hedges; watch for either (a) White House statement, (b) Commerce fact-sheet drop, or (c) selective leak to Bloomberg/Reuters as the catalyst.

headline: ALAB (Astera Labs, non-House-coverage but sentiment proxy) — BofA raises PT to $450 from $240 pre-market Monday July 6, keeps Neutral rating; ALAB +10.5% pre-market on the note; parallel read-through: BofA's $2.7T 2030 semiconductor TAM (28% CAGR 2025-2030, up from $2.3T prior) is the framework driving the July upgrade cycle and is broadly consistent with the sell-side PT resets in the House-covered AMD/MU/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC cohort last week
summary: Bank of America raised its price target on Astera Labs (ALAB) to $450 from $240 pre-market Monday July 6, 2026 while maintaining a Neutral rating on the shares. ALAB traded +10.5% pre-market on the note. ALAB is not in House coverage, but the PT reset is the freshest sell-side signal for how the analyst community is now framing the AI-connectivity/PCIe-retimer sub-sector and is directly consistent with the framework the House complex has been re-rating on for the past ten days: (1) BofA's June 23 revision of the 2030 semiconductor TAM to $2.7T from $2.3T (implying 28% CAGR 2025-2030); (2) Cantor Fitzgerald AMD $700 top-of-Street PT (Jun 29); (3) UBS AMD $670 (Jun 29); (4) Wells Fargo AMD $615 (Jul 1); (5) Cantor AMAT $850, KLAC $325, LRCX $500 (Jun 29). Read-through to House coverage: (1) POSITIVE for the AI-hardware compute complex broadly (NVDA, AMD, AVGO, ANET, MU, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) as a shared TAM framework re-anchors sell-side models; (2) POSITIVE for MRVL comparables if any House-covered names re-rate on the ALAB analog; (3) BofA's Neutral-with-higher-PT combo is a reminder that the sell-side is still hedging the outright rating even as the numbers ratchet up — not a full-throated buy call.

headline: TSMC (TSM) — June 2026 monthly revenue set to release around Friday July 10; May print NT$416.98B (+30.1% YoY, +1.5% MoM, single-month record) is the base rate; watchlist item is whether the sustain runs above +25% YoY into Q3 with BofA's 'bubble-risk' note from July 1 lurking as the alternate framing; ASML Q2 print scheduled mid-July is the follow-on cross-check
summary: Reminder / calendar item — TSMC's June 2026 monthly revenue release is scheduled around Friday July 10 (TSMC publishes on the 10th trading day of the following month). May print was NT$416.98B (+30.1% YoY, +1.5% MoM), a single-month record. The June number is the first supply-side confirmation of whether AI chip demand is holding at May's pace or beginning to normalize as BofA's July 1 bubble-risk note implied. Sell-side is calibrated for a mild deceleration from May's +30% off the tough base effect. Read-through: (1) BINARY-LITE EVENT for TSM / NVDA / AMD / AVGO / MU / AMAT / LRCX / KLAC when the print lands — a +30%-plus YoY sustain would refute the BofA bubble framing; anything below +20% would validate it and drive a second wave of chip-cohort profit-taking; (2) ASML Q2 print scheduled mid-July is the follow-on order-backlog cross-check; (3) sits alongside SK Hynix Nasdaq ADR debut July 10 and Samsung Q2 preliminary Jul 7 as the four biggest AI-cycle data points landing in the same week.
